title: morisonia frondosa
intro: Morisonia frondosa, also known as the fish poison tree or poison nut, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly due to its toxic alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Morisonia frondosa include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, although its toxicity also requires careful handling and preparation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its seeds or roots in hot water, but its use should be strictly under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
